title: suites.v1 — the validation suite declarations
description: Declares the engine's validation suites. A suite is a thin declaration: a name, the trigger that runs it, and the execution context that decides whether its failures block. It does NOT list its rules — each check rule declares which suites it joins, so a suite's roster is derived from the rules present, never hand-maintained here. This file is the validation engine's own configuration; the engine validates it against this schema on every run and stops loudly if it does not conform.

$schema.description: Optional pointer from the declarations file to this governing schema, for editor and tooling validation.

suites.description: One entry per suite, keyed by the suite's name.

trigger.description: When this suite runs. The closed v1 set: pre-commit (the commit boundary), pre-close (the end-of-session ritual), CI (the pull-request check), and audit-prep (a scheduled report). Adding a trigger is a deliberate, reviewed change, not a casual one.

context.description: What a failure does in this suite. blocking-gate = a hard failure stops the merge (only the CI check can do this); local-nudge = a hard failure is a strong warning that never blocks; report-only = failures are recorded, never block.
